sections.content: Clicking the yellow shape changed something (board_changed=True) but level not complete. Let me check what changed. The yellow shapes might have moved or changed color. Let me compare before and after.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking yellow shape
# Compare previous_frame to current_frame
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Find yellow shapes in both
prev_yellows = {n['hash']: n for n in prev_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
curr_yellows = {n['hash']: n for n in curr_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}

print("Previous yellow hashes:", list(prev_yellows.keys()))
print("Current yellow hashes:", list(curr_yellows.keys()))

# Check all nodes for changes
for n in curr_seg['nodes']:
    br = n['boundary'][0]
    # Check button area
    if 40 <= br[0] <= 47 and 18 <= br[1] <= 44:
        print(f"Button: ({br[0]}, {br[1]}): {n['color']}")

# Check yellow shapes
for n in curr_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        br = n['boundary']
        print(f"Yellow: boundary={br}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The yellow shapes and buttons are unchanged. The board changed but I can't see what. Let me check ALL nodes for changes between frames - maybe something subtle changed like the blue shape or some other element.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare all nodes between previous and current frames
prev_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in previous_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
curr_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']}

# Check for any color or position changes
print("=== Checking for changes ===")
for h, pn in prev_nodes.items():
    if h in curr_nodes:
        cn = curr_nodes[h]
        if pn['color'] != cn['color']:
            print(f"Color change hash={h}: {pn['color']} -> {cn['color']}")
        # Check position change
        if pn['boundary'] != cn['boundary']:
            print(f"Position change hash={h}: {pn['boundary']} -> {cn['boundary']}")
    else:
        print(f"Disappeared: hash={h}, color={pn['color']}")

for h, cn in curr_nodes.items():
    if h not in prev_nodes:
        print(f"Appeared: hash={h}, color={cn['color']}")

# Check blue shape
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"Blue shape: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blue shape at the top (hash changed) and gray shape changed. The top blue bar is the timer/progress bar - it's shrinking. The bottom blue shape is still there. The gray shape change is likely related to the progress bar too.
So clicking the yellow shape just advanced the timer/progress bar but didn't change gameplay. This confirms the yellow shapes are NOT interactive targets.
Let me reconsider. Both tops and stems are now B,w,w,w,B (matching the U-shape). The level is still not complete. What else could I try?
Maybe I need to click the blue shape at the bottom to "submit"? Let me try that.
